Welcome, Kayle! I’m Claude.

Bring me anything—a tough problem, a half-formed idea, something you need to write. We’ll figure it out together.

Where do you want to start?

Claude my man, I need to find a time card system that integreates out codes. Right now our employees are writing in pencil everyday what they do . example: Jorgenson Job, Framing 6115, 8-12 (4 hours) insulation VB 7201 1230-430 ( 4 hours). Our accountant has to manually input each of these line items into a software, track them per job and then update to quick books. time sheets need to be filled out in a way that they can be integrated into a excel sheet. Is there anything out there like this? would it be simple enough for us to create?

Apr 17
Searched the web

Great question — and yes, there are solid off-the-shelf solutions, AND this is absolutely something you could build yourself for a fraction of the cost. Let me break both paths down for you.

Off-the-Shelf Options

Here are the top contenders built exactly for your situation:

🏆 Workyard — Best fit for construction Employees can capture hours worked per job and cost code with every time card, and it syncs directly to QuickBooks Online. Workyard It runs about $6–13/user/month plus a $50 base fee. Workyard Your employees use the phone app, pick the job (Jorgenson Job), pick the cost code (6115 Framing), enter their hours — done. It flows straight to QuickBooks.

ClockShark — Simpler, slightly cheaper ClockShark integrates seamlessly with QuickBooks and offers job costing tools to track labor costs per project. ServiceTitan It runs about $9–11/user/month plus a $40–60 base fee. Workyard Clean interface, easy for field crews.

QuickBooks Time (built by Intuit) — Most seamless QB integration It integrates directly with QuickBooks Online, QuickBooks Payroll, and QuickBooks Desktop, with each worker running about $8–10/month depending on the plan tier. QuickBooks Since you're already in QuickBooks, this has zero friction on the accounting side.

Could You Build It Yourself?

Yes — and honestly it might be the smarter move for a smaller crew. Here's what a custom solution could look like:

  • A simple Google Form or web app on each employee's phone
  • They select: Employee name → Job name → Cost code → Start time → End time
  • All entries flow automatically into a Google Sheet
  • The sheet is formatted to export directly to Excel or connect to QuickBooks via import

The advantages of building your own:

  • Free or nearly free (Google Sheets + Forms = $0)
  • You control the cost codes — no setup fees to add new ones
  • The Excel export can be formatted exactly the way your accountant needs it
  • No monthly per-user fees

The tradeoff is it won't auto-push to QuickBooks — your accountant would still import a file, but it would be a clean, structured import instead of manual pencil transcription. That alone saves hours every week.

My Recommendation

If you have 10+ employees, go with ClockShark or Workyard — the time savings pay for themselves fast. Contractors using this type of system typically report saving 5–10 hours of admin time per week. Workyard

If you have a smaller crew (under 10), I can build you a custom time card web app right now — employees fill it out on their phone, it stores everything, and exports a clean Excel sheet formatted for QuickBooks import. Want me to do that?

how hard would it be to build an app in house? feesable?

Apr 17

Totally feasible — and honestly not as hard as you might think. Let me give you a realistic picture.

What You'd Actually Need

The core of what you're describing is pretty simple:

  • A form employees fill out on their phone (job, cost code, start/end time)
  • A database that stores all the entries
  • An export that spits out a clean Excel file for your accountant

That's it. No GPS tracking, no payroll engine, no complex logic.

The Honest Difficulty Scale

Option A — Google Forms + Sheets (Easy, free, 1 day to set up) You could have something working today. Employees fill out a Google Form on their phone, it dumps into a spreadsheet automatically, and your accountant gets a clean export. The downside is it's not very polished and has no login system.

Option B — Custom Web App (Moderate, low cost, 1–2 weeks to build) A proper app with a login, dropdown menus for jobs and cost codes, a time picker, and a clean Excel export. This is what I could build for you right here as a starting point. Employees open it on their phone browser — no app store, no downloads. It would use persistent storage so nothing gets lost.

Option C — Native Mobile App (Hard, expensive, months) Not worth it for your use case. Skip this entirely.

What Makes It Easy In Your Case

Your workflow is actually very simple data:

Employee + Job + Cost Code + Date + Start Time + End Time = one row

That's one database record. Everything else — totals, job summaries, QuickBooks export — is just math on top of that. There's no complicated logic here.

What It Would Cost

PathCostTimeTech Needed
Google Forms/Sheets$01 dayNone
Custom web app (I build it)$0Right nowNone
Hire a developer$2,000–8,0004–8 weeksFind someone
Off-the-shelf (ClockShark etc.)$100–300/mo foreverSetup dayNone

My Honest Take

For a construction crew your size, a custom web app is 100% feasible. The data model is simple, the employees just need dropdowns on their phone, and the output is a spreadsheet.

I can build you a working prototype right now — you'd be able to open it on your phone, add a time entry with job name, cost code, start/end time, and export it to Excel. You could test it with your crew this week and see if it fits before spending a dime on anything.

Want me to go ahead and build it?

ya build it

Apr 17

V

visualize

V

visualize show_widget

There's your working app. Here's what it does:

Log time tab — employees pick their name, job, cost code, date, and start/end times. Hours calculate automatically. Takes about 30 seconds to fill out.

Entries tab — shows all logged time with totals. Filter by employee or job. Hit "Export Excel" and it downloads a clean .xlsx file, one row per entry, formatted exactly how an accountant can import or sort.

Jobs & codes tab — add/remove employees, jobs, and cost codes anytime. Your example codes (6115 Framing, 7201 Insulation VB) are already in there to show you how it looks.

To make this permanent and shareable across your whole crew, the next step would be to host it as a simple web page — your guys just bookmark the link on their phone. That would cost around $0–10/month on something like Netlify or a basic web host, and I can set that up for you too. Want me to package it up for that?

index.html — your complete app, one file, no dependencies needed.

HOW-TO-DEPLOY.md — step-by-step instructions, but honestly the fastest path is:

  1. Go to netlify.com/drop
  2. Drag index.html onto the page
  3. You get a live URL in about 30 seconds — totally free

Your crew then bookmarks that URL on their phones and it looks and works like an app. On iPhone they can even add it to their home screen so it has its own icon.

One thing to know about this version: data saves to each phone individually, so employees export to Excel at the end of the week and send it to your accountant. If you want all crew entries to go into one central place automatically (so your accountant can just pull a report anytime), that's the next upgrade — takes a small cloud database but still very cheap. Just say the word when you're ready for that.

Got it — clean and clear. Now I'll build the digital version to match exactly: same columns (Date, Project, Labour Code, Activity, Start Time, Finish Time, Total Hours), Employee Name and Month at the top, Total Hours at the bottom, and all your RCS codes in a dropdown.

Created a file, read a file

There's your RCS timesheet app. Here's what it does:

Timesheet tab — matches your exact column layout: Date, Project, Labour Code, Activity, Start Time, Finish Time, Total Hours. Employee Name and Month at the top, Total Hours tallied at the bottom. Hours calculate automatically when you enter start and finish times.

Labour Codes tab — all 50 of your RCS codes are searchable. Tap any code and it drops straight into your timesheet. Workers can pull this up while filling out their sheet if they can't remember a code number.

Export to Excel — spits out a .xlsx file named RCS_Timesheet_[Name]_[Month].xlsx with the employee name, month, and all rows including the total — ready for your accountant.

To get it live, just drag that index.html to netlify.com/drop and you'll have a URL to share with your crew in under a minute. Want me to also build the deployment instructions file again, or are you good to go?

I need this to be super user friendly on a mobile device. how to get to it on my mobible device?

Apr 17

Great question — two steps:

Step 1 — Get it online (2 minutes)

  1. Go to netlify.com/drop on your computer
  2. Drag the index.html file onto the page
  3. You'll get a free URL like https://fuzzy-crane-123.netlify.app

Step 2 — Add it to your phone's home screen so it feels like an app

iPhone:

  1. Open Safari and go to your URL
  2. Tap the Share button (box with arrow at the bottom)
  3. Scroll down and tap "Add to Home Screen"
  4. Name it "RCS Timesheets" → tap Add
  5. It now sits on your home screen with its own icon — opens like an app, no browser bar

Android:

  1. Open Chrome and go to your URL
  2. Tap the 3-dot menu (top right)
  3. Tap "Add to Home Screen"
  4. Name it and tap Add

That said — before you do any of that, let me rebuild the app first so it's actually designed for mobile. Right now the table layout works fine on a desktop but on a phone screen those 7 columns will be cramped.

I can rebuild it so on mobile it shows each entry as a card (one entry per screen, big tap-friendly inputs, large dropdown for the codes) and on desktop it still shows the familiar table view your accountant sees. Best of both worlds.

Want me to do that rebuild first before you deploy it?

asking for a password

Apr 17

Good catch — that's Netlify asking you to create an account. You don't need to. Here's how to skip it:

  1. Go to netlify.com/drop
  2. Drag your file onto the page
  3. When it asks you to sign up — look for the small text that says "Skip this step" or "Continue without an account" at the bottom of the popup
  4. It'll still give you a live URL without needing a password

If you can't find that option, the dead-simple alternative is:

Tiiny.host — literally the easiest option

  1. Go to tiiny.host
  2. Click "Upload"
  3. Drop your index.html file
  4. Done — you get a free URL instantly, no account needed

That URL is what you share with your crew and add to your home screen. Want to try that one?

Here's the mobile-first rebuild. Here's what's different:

Designed for thumbs — every input is large, well spaced, and easy to tap. No tiny table cells to poke at.

Three tabs at the bottom (just like a real app):

  • Sheet — your entries, one card per row, with big dropdowns for project and labour code
  • Codes — all 50 RCS codes with a live search bar. Tap any code and it drops straight into your sheet

Your name — tap the pill in the top right to set your name once, it stays saved.

  2. Open that URL in your phone's browser
  3. iPhone: tap Share → "Add to Home Screen" → it sits on your home screen like an app
  4. Android: tap ⋮ → "Add to Home Screen"

Your crew does the same — you just text them the link.

making me pay to fill it out.

Apr 17

That's Tiiny.host paywalling you. Let's use a completely free option instead.

Try Netlify — it's genuinely free, no credit card:

  1. Go to app.netlify.com
  2. Click "Sign up" — use your Google or GitHub account (no credit card ever asked)
  3. Once in, look for "Sites" and drag your index.html file right onto the page
  4. You get a free URL instantly

Or even simpler — GitHub Pages (100% free forever):

  1. Go to github.com and create a free account
  2. Click the + button → "New repository"
  3. Name it rcs-timesheet, make it Public, click Create
  4. Click "uploading an existing file" → drag in your index.html → click Commit
  5. Go to Settings → Pages → under Source select "main" branch → click Save
  6. Your URL will be: https://yourusername.github.io/rcs-timesheet

That URL works forever for free and you can share it with your whole crew.

Want me to walk you through it step by step? If you tell me which option you want to try I can give you exact screenshots-level instructions.

Apr 17

You're on the wrong page — that's the "Import repository" page which is for copying code from another website. You don't need that.

Here's exactly what to do instead:

Step 1 — Go to: github.com/new

That takes you to the "Create a new repository" page (much simpler than what you're seeing)

Step 2 — Fill it in:

  • Repository name: rcs-timesheet
  • Select Public
  • Leave everything else blank
  • Click "Create repository"

Step 3 — On the next page, look for the link that says "uploading an existing file" and click that

Step 4 — Drag your index.html file onto the upload box → click "Commit changes"

Step 5 — Click Settings (top of the page) → scroll down to Pages on the left menu → under "Branch" select main → click Save

Step 6 — Wait 1-2 minutes, then your URL will be: https://swannykay.github.io/rcs-timesheet

The key thing — go to github.com/new not the import page. Want to share a screenshot once you're on that page and I'll guide you through it?

Apr 17

The site is live but the file has a capitalization issue. GitHub is case-sensitive — it needs the file to be named exactly index.html (all lowercase).

Let's fix it in 3 steps:

Step 1 — Go to: github.com/swannykay/rcs-timesheet

Step 2 — Click on your file in the list. What is it named exactly? It might say Index.html or index (1).html — let me know what you see and I'll tell you exactly what to click to rename or re-upload it correctly.

If you see the file is named anything other than index.html, here's the fix:

  1. Click the file
  2. Click the pencil icon (Edit) in the top right
  3. At the top, change the filename to exactly index.html
  4. Scroll down and click "Commit changes"

Then wait 1 minute and try https://swannykay.github.io/rcs-timesheet again. What's the file named?

Apr 17

Found it! The file is named index (1).html — that's the problem. It needs to be renamed to exactly index.html. Here's how:

1. Click on index (1).html in the file list

2. Click the pencil icon ✏️ in the top right of the file view

3. At the very top you'll see the filename in a box — delete what's there and type exactly: index.html

4. Scroll down and click the green "Commit changes" button → click "Commit changes" again in the popup

5. Wait 1 minute then try: https://swannykay.github.io/rcs-timesheet

That should fix it!

working. How easy is this to edit and create new job names?

Apr 17

Super easy! Here's how it works:

On the app itself (for everyone):

  • Tap the Setup tab (person icon, bottom right)
  • Tap "Add" next to Projects to add a new job name
  • It saves instantly and shows up in the dropdown on the Sheet tab
  • Tap × to remove any job you no longer need

Each person on your crew can add jobs on their own phone — takes about 5 seconds.

The only limitation with the current setup is that each phone saves its own list. So if you add "Smith Renovation" on your phone, Mike's phone won't automatically see it.

Two ways around that:

  1. You manage the master list — you add all job names in Setup, then when a new job starts you text the crew "new job added, re-bookmark the link" — they just refresh and it's there. (Doesn't actually work this way with local storage though)
  2. The real fix — I can update the app so the job list is hardcoded and always the same for everyone. You'd just tell me your job names and I update the file on GitHub. Takes 2 minutes.

Honestly option 2 is the cleanest for a crew — want me to add all your current job names into the app right now? Just send me the list and I'll update it.

Good call — 15 minute increments keeps it clean and consistent. And 10 rows per day is plenty for most crews.

Quick question before I build it:

Once you answer those I'll build it and give you the updated file to drop straight into GitHub — one upload and it's live for your whole crew.

Q: How should time be entered? A: Clock picker that snaps to nearest 15 min

Q: Should each DAY have its own section, or just 10 rows total on the sheet? A: Each day gets its own section with 10 rows

Q: Should the app pre-fill today's date automatically? A: Yes, default to today

15-minute time increments — Start and Finish are now dropdowns showing clean slots like 6:00 AM, 6:15 AM, 6:30 AM... all the way to 10:00 PM. No typing, no wrong formats, just tap and select.

10 tasks per day — Each day is its own card with 10 task rows. Each row has Start, Finish, Project, Labour Code, and Activity. Hours calculate automatically per row and total up at the top of each day card.

can we change the name of the website so it shorter and doesnt have swannkyay in it

Apr 17

Yes easily! You have two options:

Option 1 — Rename the repository (free, 2 minutes) This changes the URL to something like swannykay.github.io/rcs or swannykay.github.io/time — shorter but still has your username in it.

  1. Go to github.com/swannykay/rcs-timesheet
  2. Click Settings
  3. At the top under "Repository name" — change it to something short like rcs or time
  4. Click Rename
  5. New URL becomes: swannykay.github.io/rcs

Option 2 — Custom domain like rcstime.com (~$15/year) This gives you a completely clean URL with no GitHub in it at all — something like:

  • rcstime.ca
  • rococotime.ca
  • rcsfield.ca

You buy the domain from somewhere like Namecheap.com (~$15/yr) and point it at GitHub. Takes about 20 minutes to set up and I can walk you through every step.

Which direction do you want to go? Just shorter, or a proper custom domain with your company name?

ok one more issue, i want it to start with one task on the day. then have an option to add another task that day or add new day. also i will have 15 employees using this. can each one save there data or how will this work? they have to fill it out that day and make sure its fully completed before it is exported?

Apr 17

Great questions — let me tackle both:

On the 1 task to start + add more: Easy fix — each day starts with 1 row, and there's an "+ Add task" button under it. Way cleaner than 10 blank rows staring at them.

On 15 employees — this is the important one:

Right now each phone saves its own data locally. That actually works perfectly for your setup — here's why:

  • John opens the app on his phone → fills out his day → exports his Excel at end of week → sends it to your accountant
  • Mike does the same on his phone
  • They never interfere with each other

The workflow would be:

  1. Each employee sets their name once (tap the pill top right)
  2. They fill out their sheet daily
  3. Friday afternoon — hit Export → file goes to your accountant via text or email
  4. Accountant gets 15 clean spreadsheets, imports to QuickBooks

The one rule — they need to export before clearing their browser or getting a new phone, since data lives on the device.

Want me to also add a "Send to accountant" email button that fires off their export directly to your accountant's email? They tap one button and it's gone — zero friction.

didnt update?

Apr 17

My fault — the file on GitHub still has the old version. You need to replace it manually. Here's the quickest way:

  1. Go to github.com/swannykay/rcs-timesheet
  2. Click on index.html
  3. Click the pencil icon ✏️ to edit
  4. Press Ctrl+A to select all the code
  5. Press Delete to clear it
  6. Now open the index.html file I just gave you on your computer — open it in Notepad (right-click the file → Open with → Notepad)
  7. Press Ctrl+A then Ctrl+C to copy everything
  8. Go back to GitHub and Ctrl+V to paste
  9. Scroll down and click Commit changes → Commit changes

Wait 60 seconds and it'll be live.

The copy/paste method is the most reliable way to update it. Want me to also look at making this easier for future updates?

iPhone Safari: Close the tab completely, reopen and go to the URL again Android Chrome: Tap ⋮ → tap the refresh icon, or hold the refresh button and tap "Hard reload"

Sometimes the phone caches the old version. Let me know what you see!

  4. Click Commit changes
  5. Then click Add file → Upload files
  6. Drag the index.html file I gave you onto the page
  7. Click Commit changes

That completely replaces it with no cache issues. The trash + re-upload is cleaner than editing in place.

while were here can i change the name of this website ? so swannykay is not incororated. or should i create a different account

Apr 17

Easiest thing is to just create a brand new GitHub account under your company name — that way the URL looks clean and professional for your whole crew.

Here's what I'd suggest:

  1. Go to github.com and create a new account
    • Username: rococoltd or rocococonstruction or rcsjobs
    • That makes your URL: https://rococoltd.github.io/rcs-timesheet
  2. Create the same repository (rcs-timesheet), upload the index.html
  3. Turn on GitHub Pages the same way you did before
  4. New clean URL would be something like: https://rococoltd.github.io/rcs-timesheet

No trace of Swannykay anywhere — looks like a proper company tool.

Or even cleaner — if you want something like just rcstime.ca or rococotime.ca that's a custom domain for about $15/year. That hides GitHub completely and just looks like your own app.

Which direction feels right — new GitHub account, or a proper custom domain?

Can we make it so when your first task was over at 1200 the time refresh and you can only have from 1200 on.. that make sense? no point having anything before 12 that day if you've already worked till lunch have the latest time in the day being 8pm and earliest 530 am

Apr 17

Makes total sense — each task's finish time becomes the earliest start time for the next task. No overlapping, no going backwards. Let me build that in.

Ran a command, edited a file

Now update the row render to pass the minTime for start, and finish must be after start:

Update row render to use smart time constraints

Now update updRow so when a finish time is set, the next row's start auto-populates:

Edited a file, ran a command, read a file

Here's the updated file. Here's exactly how it works now:

  • Time range — 5:30 AM to 8:00 PM only, in 15 min increments
  • Task 1 — start can be anything from 5:30 AM onward, finish must be after start
  • Task 2 — start dropdown automatically begins at whatever Task 1's finish was. If Task 1 finished at 12:00 PM, Task 2 can only start at 12:00 PM or later
  • Auto-fill — when you set a finish time, the next task's start automatically jumps to that time so they don't even have to select it
  • No going backwards — impossible to accidentally overlap tasks

Upload this to github.com/RococoConstruction/time the same way — drag and replace the file. Live in 60 seconds!

not sending all the details like we need on excel for tina to get all the information. it downloads it and then we have to resend the email which come great. can we make it work together without the multiple steps?

Apr 17

Totally understand — the problem is that phones won't let a browser automatically attach a file to an email, it's a security restriction. So we can't do it in one tap on mobile.

The cleanest solution is to skip email entirely and have it go straight to Tina automatically with no employee involvement at all. To do that properly we need a tiny backend — but there's a free and simple way to do it using Google Sheets + Apps Script.

Here's how it would work:

  • Employee taps "Send to Tina"
  • All their timesheet data gets sent directly into a Google Sheet that Tina, Kayle and Darren can see in real time
  • No email, no attachment, no extra steps — just one tap and it's done
  • Tina opens the Google Sheet and sees every employee's entries already formatted and ready

From Tina's side — she'd just open one Google Sheet and see all 15 employees' timesheets come in, filter by name or date, and export to Excel whenever she wants for QuickBooks.

This is honestly the better solution for 15 employees — everything goes to one central place automatically.

Want me to build that? It takes about 10 minutes to set up and is completely free using Google's tools.

Let's do the google sheet please

Apr 17

Perfect. This will be the cleanest solution. Here's the plan before I build it:

How it will work:

  1. Employee fills out their timesheet and taps "Send to Tina"
  2. Data goes instantly and automatically into a Google Sheet
  3. Tina, Kayle and Darren open the sheet anytime and see everyone's entries live
  4. They filter by name, date, or job and export to Excel for QuickBooks whenever they want

To set it up I need you to do one thing first:

  1. Go to sheets.google.com
  2. Create a new blank spreadsheet
  3. Name it RCS Timesheets
  4. Click Extensions in the top menu → Apps Script
  5. Take a screenshot and send it to me — I'll give you the exact code to paste in that connects your app to the sheet

This creates the "receiver" that accepts data from your employees' phones. Takes about 5 minutes and it's completely free.

Can you get to that step?

No reply?

10:00 a.m.

Sorry about that! Still here.

Go ahead and follow these steps:

  1. Go to sheets.google.com
  2. Create a blank spreadsheet
  3. Name it RCS Timesheets
  4. Click Extensions in the top menu → Apps Script

Take a screenshot when you have the Apps Script window open and I'll give you the exact code to paste in. That's the piece that connects your app to the sheet!

12:40 p.m.

Perfect — you're in the right place! Now:

  1. Select all the code in there (click in the editor, press Ctrl+A)
  2. Delete it
  3. Paste this code in:
javascript
function doPost(e) {
  try {
    var sheet = SpreadsheetApp.getActiveSpreadsheet().getActiveSheet();
    var data = JSON.parse(e.postData.contents);
    
    // Add header row if sheet is empty
    if (sheet.getLastRow() === 0) {
      sheet.appendRow(['Submitted', 'Employee', 'Month', 'Date', 'Project', 'Labour Code', 'Code Description', 'Hours', 'Break', 'Break Note']);
      sheet.getRange(1, 1, 1, 10).setFontWeight('bold');
    }
    
    // Append each row
    data.rows.forEach(function(row) {
      sheet.appendRow([
        new Date().toLocaleString(),
        data.emp,
        data.month,
        row.date,
        row.proj,
        row.code,
        row.codeName,
        row.hours,
        data.breakDur || '',
        data.breakNote || ''
      ]);
    });
    
    return ContentService.createTextOutput(JSON.stringify({status:'ok'})).setMimeType(ContentService.MimeType.JSON);
  } catch(err) {
    return ContentService.createTextOutput(JSON.stringify({status:'error',message:err.toString()})).setMimeType(ContentService.MimeType.JSON);
  }
}
  1. Click the 💾 Save icon (or Ctrl+S)
  2. Then click the Deploy button (top right, blue button)

Take a screenshot of the Deploy screen and I'll walk you through the next step!

explain better again please

12:41 p.m.

Sure! Here's exactly what to do step by step:

Step 1 — Click inside the code editor where you see function myFunction() {

Step 2 — Press Ctrl+A on your keyboard to select all that text

Step 3 — Press Delete to clear it out — the editor should now be completely blank

Step 4 — Copy the code I gave you above (click on it, Ctrl+A, Ctrl+C)

Step 5 — Click back in the Apps Script editor and press Ctrl+V to paste it in

Step 6 — Press Ctrl+S to save — you'll see the title change from "Untitled project" to saved

Step 7 — Click the blue Deploy button in the top right corner

Send me a screenshot after you click Deploy and I'll guide you through the last couple of steps to get it connected to your app!

12:44 p.m.

The code is in perfectly! Now click "New deployment" from that dropdown menu you have open right now.

OK

12:45 p.m.

You should see a dialog box now. Here's what to do:

  1. Click the gear/settings icon ⚙️ next to "Select type"
  2. Select "Web app"
  3. Fill it in like this:
    • Description: RCS Timesheets
    • Execute as: Me
    • Who has access: Anyone
  4. Click Deploy

Take a screenshot of what you see and I'll guide you through the next step!

12:46 p.m.

Click "Authorize access" — this just lets the script write to your Google Sheet. It's safe, it's your own account.

ok

12:46 p.m.

You'll see a "Choose an account" screen — click your Google account (the one you used to make the sheet).

Then it may say "Google hasn't verified this app" — that's normal for scripts you write yourself. Click "Advanced" then click "Go to Untitled project (unsafe)" — it's safe, it's your own code.

Then click "Allow".

Screenshot what you see next!

12:46 p.m.

This is the warning I mentioned — it's completely normal and safe since it's your own script.

Click "Advanced" (bottom left of that popup) then click "Go to Untitled project (unsafe)" — don't worry about the word "unsafe", it just means Google hasn't reviewed it, but it's your own code!

12:47 p.m.
